title: impatiens noli-tangere subsp. biflora
intro: Impatiens noli-tangere subsp. biflora is a perennial plant species belonging to the balsam family, also known as the touch-me-not plant, which is native to Asia and parts of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Impatiens noli-tangere subsp. biflora include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
